channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Fire outbreak destroys millions worth of goods at Head Bridge Market plumbing section in Onitsha
item.description: A serious fire outbreak happened for early hours of Saturday for Head Bridge Market, specifically the Plumbing Material Section, near Ogbo-Ogwu Medicine Market for Onitsha, Anambra State commercial hu...

item.title: Shashi Tharoor Acknowledges Low Women’s Representation in Kerala Congress Candidates, Cites Winnability as Key Factor
item.description: Congress leader Shashi Tharoor has supported the demand for greater representation of women in elections while responding to concerns raised by party colleague Shama Mohamed regarding the low number o...

item.title: Kedipa Proposes Disability Inclusion Audits for All Laws in Kenya
item.description: The Kenya Disability Parliamentary Association (KEDIPA) has called for mandatory disability inclusion audits for all laws, marking a significant shift in how Kenya develops its legislation. The propos...

item.title: Israel intensifies attacks on Tehran and Beirut as US deploys Marines to Middle East
item.description: In a significant escalation of the conflict in the Middle East, Israel launched airstrikes on Tehran and Beirut on March 21, 2026, as the US sent thousands of Marines to the region. This comes after t...

item.title: US Jury Holds Elon Musk Liable for Misleading Twitter Shareholders in 2022 Acquisition Case
item.description: A federal jury in San Francisco has ruled that Elon Musk, the world's richest person, is liable for defrauding Twitter shareholders by making misleading statements on social media that artificially lo...

item.title: Israel Conducts Airstrikes on Tehran Following Beirut Operations
item.description: In a significant escalation of the ongoing Middle East conflict, the Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) launched a series of airstrikes targeting Iranian government infrastructure in Tehran, shortly after c...

item.title: Wall Street Declines Sharply Amid Prolonged US-Israel War on Iran and Rising Inflation Concerns
item.description: Wall Street witnessed a sharp decline on Friday, with major indices closing lower due to escalating concerns over the ongoing US-Israeli war against Iran, now in its fourth week. The S&P 500 fell 1.51...

item.title: Goulburn Sunday horse racing tips and race previews from John Schell
item.description: This article provides detailed tips and previews for the nine-race meeting at Goulburn on Sunday, with selections based on a soft track. In Race 1, debutant Zou Force is tipped to win after strong tri...

item.title: Alberta to close supervised consumption sites in Calgary and Lethbridge as recovery services expand
item.description: The Alberta government has announced the closure of two supervised drug consumption sites in Calgary and Lethbridge effective June 30, 2026. This decision reduces the number of remaining sites in the ...

item.title: Middle East War Impact Threatens Africa's $155bn Borrowing Forecast
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East is casting a shadow over Africa's plans to tap international capital markets for $155 billion in borrowing. According to S&P Global Ratings, the war's impact on...

item.title: Iran attempts missile strike on UK-US base at Diego Garcia amid escalating conflict
item.description: Iran has launched two intermediate-range ballistic missiles towards the joint UK-US military base on Diego Garcia in the Indian Ocean, though neither struck the target. According to reports, one missi...

item.title: Oscar Sudi Reunites with Primary School Crush and Pledges Support for Her Daughter
item.description: Kapseret MP Oscar Sudi recently shared a nostalgic and heartwarming moment during a public event when he introduced his primary school girlfriend, Ruth. He openly admitted that he had a crush on her d...

item.title: Delhi Experiences Cold Spell: Minimum Temperature Falls to 13°C Amid Fog
item.description: Delhi woke up to a chilly Saturday (March 21, 2026) with a significant drop in the minimum temperature, registering a low of 13 degrees Celsius. This is 3.5 degrees below the usual, and accompanied by...

item.title: Police Invite Acting Chairman of Etsako East LG and Three Officials Over Alleged N100 Million Fraud
item.description: The Police Special Fraud Unit (PSFU) don invite the Acting Chairman of Etsako East Local Government Area for Edo State, Sunday Akiotu, plus three big officials from the council over serious allegation...

item.title: Three Young African Economists Selected as Finalists for Inaugural Africa NextGen Economist Prize 2026
item.description: The Africa NextGen Economist Prize, organised by Jeune Afrique and The Africa Report together with the African Development Bank, has named its three finalists for the 2026 inaugural edition. From more...

item.title: US temporarily lifts sanctions on Iranian oil at sea for 30 days amid West Asia conflict to ease global supply pressures
item.description: The United States has temporarily lifted sanctions for 30 days on the purchase of Iranian crude oil and petroleum products currently at sea. This move aims to bring approximately 140 million barrels o...

item.title: Clearing Agents Lament NPA Delays in Processing Clearing and Forwarding Licences
item.description: Clearing agents wey dey operate for Nigerian ports don seriously complain about di Nigerian Ports Authority (NPA) silence on plenty operational licences wey dem never process. According to Mr. Emmanue...

item.title: UK Scientists Successfully Transplant Lab-Grown Esophagus in Pigs, Paving Way for Treatments in Children with Esophageal Atresia
item.description: Researchers from Great Ormond Street Hospital (GOSH) and University College London (UCL) have achieved a significant milestone in regenerative medicine by creating the first fully functional lab-grown...

item.title: TGNP Forum in Dar es Salaam Promotes Economic Inclusion for Youth and Persons with Disabilities

item.title: African Women Leading the Global Rise of Afrobeats, Amapiano, Bongo Flava and Other Genres
item.description: African women have played a big role in putting the continent's music on the world map for many years. From the old days of Afro-jazz and soul to today's big explosion of Afrobeats, Amapiano and Bongo...
